message:
Fix multi-dimensional VJP slice adjoint, harden dtype promotion in select, and improve correctness across AD/LAX/egraph

- Rewrite VJP backward pass for slice operations to correctly handle
  multi-dimensional tensors by computing N-dimensional input coordinates
  from output coordinates, fixing incorrect 1D-only adjoint scattering
- Add dtype promotion to eval_select for both scalar and tensor paths,
  ensuring mixed-type branches (e.g. i64 true-branch, f64 false-branch)
  produce correctly promoted output values
- Guard e-graph optimizer to bail out on multi-output jaxprs since
  tuple-node extraction is not yet implemented
- Fix partial_eval fast-path to only activate when there are no
  constvars to thread as residuals, preventing dropped constant bindings
- Pre-allocate interpreter environment HashMap with known capacity
- Skip TTL timestamp insertion in cache when ttl_secs is 0
- Use pattern matching instead of method probing in conformance type
  fingerprinting for exhaustive Literal variant coverage
- Fix element count comparison cast direction in TensorValue::new
- Pre-allocate element Vec in TensorValue::axis_stack
- Handle empty element slice in infer_dtype_from_literals
- Return scalar (not clone of tensor value) for rank-0 full reductions
- Remove .max(1) from gather/scatter slice element calculation to
  correctly handle zero-size slices
- Clamp hardened-mode kill_if_useful to minimum of 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
